member.name: T:PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.BashNode
member.summary: Base type for all nodes in the bash AST.

member.name: T:PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.Command
member.summary: Base type for all command nodes. Modeled after oils syntax.asdl command variants.

member.name: T:PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.Command.Simple
member.summary: A simple command: words with optional environment pairs and redirects. Modeled after oils command.Simple.
A simple command: words with optional environment pairs and redirects. Modeled after oils command.Simple.

member.name: T:PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.Command.Pipeline
member.summary: A pipeline of commands connected by | or |&. Modeled after oils command.Pipeline.

member.name: M:PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.Command.Pipeline.#ctor(System.Collections.Immutable.ImmutableArray{PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.Command},System.Collections.Immutable.ImmutableArray{System.String},System.Boolean)
member.summary: A pipeline of commands connected by | or |&. Modeled after oils command.Pipeline.

member.name: T:PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.Command.AndOrList
member.summary: Commands joined by && or ||. Modeled after oils command.AndOr.

member.name: M:PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.Command.AndOrList.#ctor(System.Collections.Immutable.ImmutableArray{PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.Command},System.Collections.Immutable.ImmutableArray{System.String})
member.summary: Commands joined by && or ||. Modeled after oils command.AndOr.

member.name: T:PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.Command.CommandList
member.summary: A list of commands separated by ; or newline. Modeled after oils command.CommandList.

member.name: M:PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.Command.CommandList.#ctor(System.Collections.Immutable.ImmutableArray{PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.Command})
member.summary: A list of commands separated by ; or newline. Modeled after oils command.CommandList.

member.name: T:PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.Command.ShAssignment
member.summary: A bare assignment command, e.g. x=1 y=2. Modeled after oils command.ShAssignment.

member.name: M:PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.Command.ShAssignment.#ctor(System.Collections.Immutable.ImmutableArray{PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.Assignment},System.Boolean)
member.summary: A bare assignment command, e.g. x=1 y=2. Modeled after oils command.ShAssignment.

member.name: T:PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.Command.If
member.summary: An if/elif/else statement. Modeled after oils command.If.

member.name: M:PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.Command.If.#ctor(System.Collections.Immutable.ImmutableArray{PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.IfArm},PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.Command)
member.summary: An if/elif/else statement. Modeled after oils command.If.

member.name: T:PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.Command.BoolExpr
member.summary: A test expression: [ ... ] or [[ ... ]]. The inner words are stored without the surrounding brackets.

member.name: M:PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.Command.BoolExpr.#ctor(System.Collections.Immutable.ImmutableArray{PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.CompoundWord},System.Boolean)
member.summary: A test expression: [ ... ] or [[ ... ]]. The inner words are stored without the surrounding brackets.

member.name: T:PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.Command.ForIn
member.summary: A for-in loop: for x in a b c; do body; done. An empty list means implicit $@ (for x; do ...).

member.name: M:PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.Command.ForIn.#ctor(System.String,System.Collections.Immutable.ImmutableArray{PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.CompoundWord},PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.Command)
member.summary: A for-in loop: for x in a b c; do body; done. An empty list means implicit $@ (for x; do ...).

member.name: T:PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.Command.ForArith
member.summary: A C-style arithmetic for loop: for ((init; cond; step)); do body; done. The three clauses are stored as raw strings.

member.name: M:PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.Command.ForArith.#ctor(System.String,System.String,System.String,PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.Command)
member.summary: A C-style arithmetic for loop: for ((init; cond; step)); do body; done. The three clauses are stored as raw strings.

member.name: T:PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.Command.While
member.summary: A while or until loop: while cmd; do body; done / until cmd; do body; done. When is true, the condition is logically negated.

member.name: M:PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.Command.While.#ctor(System.Boolean,PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.Command,PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.Command)
member.summary: A while or until loop: while cmd; do body; done / until cmd; do body; done. When is true, the condition is logically negated.

member.name: T:PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.Command.Case
member.summary: A case/esac statement: case expr in pattern) body;; esac. Maps to PowerShell switch.

member.name: M:PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.Command.Case.#ctor(PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.CompoundWord,System.Collections.Immutable.ImmutableArray{PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.CaseArm})
member.summary: A case/esac statement: case expr in pattern) body;; esac. Maps to PowerShell switch.

member.name: T:PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.Command.ArithCommand
member.summary: A standalone arithmetic command: (( expr )). Maps to PowerShell arithmetic expression or comparison.

member.name: M:PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.Command.ArithCommand.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: A standalone arithmetic command: (( expr )). Maps to PowerShell arithmetic expression or comparison.

member.name: T:PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.Command.ShFunction
member.summary: A function definition: function name { body } or name() { body }. Maps to PowerShell function name { body }.

member.name: M:PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.Command.ShFunction.#ctor(System.String,PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.Command)
member.summary: A function definition: function name { body } or name() { body }. Maps to PowerShell function name { body }.

member.name: T:PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.Command.Subshell
member.summary: A subshell: (cmd1; cmd2) runs commands in a child process. Maps to PowerShell & { cmd1; cmd2 }.

member.name: M:PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.Command.Subshell.#ctor(PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.Command,System.Collections.Immutable.ImmutableArray{PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.Redirect})
member.summary: A subshell: (cmd1; cmd2) runs commands in a child process. Maps to PowerShell & { cmd1; cmd2 }.

member.name: T:PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.Command.BraceGroup
member.summary: A brace group: { cmd1; cmd2; } runs commands in the current shell. Emitted inline (passthrough, same scope).

member.name: M:PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.Command.BraceGroup.#ctor(PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.Command)
member.summary: A brace group: { cmd1; cmd2; } runs commands in the current shell. Emitted inline (passthrough, same scope).

member.name: T:PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.Command.Background
member.summary: A background command: cmd & runs the inner command asynchronously. The & operator is consumed in ParseList() and wraps the preceding command in this node.

member.name: M:PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.Command.Background.#ctor(PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.Command)
member.summary: A background command: cmd & runs the inner command asynchronously. The & operator is consumed in ParseList() and wraps the preceding command in this node.

member.name: T:PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.IfArm
member.summary: A single arm of an if/elif chain: condition plus body.

member.name: M:PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.IfArm.#ctor(PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.Command,PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.Command)
member.summary: A single arm of an if/elif chain: condition plus body.

member.name: T:PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.CaseArm
member.summary: A single arm of a case/esac statement: one or more patterns and a body.

member.name: M:PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.CaseArm.#ctor(System.Collections.Immutable.ImmutableArray{System.String},PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.Command)
member.summary: A single arm of a case/esac statement: one or more patterns and a body.

member.name: T:PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.Redirect
member.summary: A redirect operation, e.g. >file, 2>&1. Modeled after oils syntax.asdl Redir.

member.name: M:PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.Redirect.#ctor(System.String,System.Int32,PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.CompoundWord)
member.summary: A redirect operation, e.g. >file, 2>&1. Modeled after oils syntax.asdl Redir.

member.name: T:PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.HereDoc
member.summary: A here-document redirect, e.g. <<EOF\ntext\nEOF. is the collected text between delimiters. is true when variable expansion should occur (unquoted delimiter). is true for <<- (leading tabs stripped from body).

member.name: M:PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.HereDoc.#ctor(System.String,System.Boolean,System.Boolean)
member.summary: A here-document redirect, e.g. <<EOF\ntext\nEOF. is the collected text between delimiters. is true when variable expansion should occur (unquoted delimiter). is true for <<- (leading tabs stripped from body).

member.name: T:PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.AssignOp
member.summary: Assignment operator: = or +=.

member.name: T:PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.Assignment
member.summary: A variable assignment, e.g. foo=bar or foo+=baz. For array assignments like arr=(a b c), is set and is null. Modeled after oils syntax.asdl AssignPair.

member.name: M:PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.Assignment.#ctor(System.String,PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.AssignOp,PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.CompoundWord,PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.ArrayWord)
member.summary: A variable assignment, e.g. foo=bar or foo+=baz. For array assignments like arr=(a b c), is set and is null. Modeled after oils syntax.asdl AssignPair.

member.name: T:PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.EnvPair
member.summary: An environment pair for command prefix, e.g. FOO=bar cmd. Modeled after oils syntax.asdl EnvPair.

member.name: M:PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.EnvPair.#ctor(System.String,PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.CompoundWord)
member.summary: An environment pair for command prefix, e.g. FOO=bar cmd. Modeled after oils syntax.asdl EnvPair.

member.name: T:PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.WordPart
member.summary: Base type for all word parts. A word part is a component of a compound word. Modeled after oils syntax.asdl word_part variants.

member.name: T:PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.WordPart.Literal
member.summary: An unquoted literal string, e.g. hello.

member.name: M:PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.WordPart.Literal.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: An unquoted literal string, e.g. hello.

member.name: T:PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.WordPart.EscapedLiteral
member.summary: A backslash-escaped character, e.g. \ (escaped space).

member.name: M:PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.WordPart.EscapedLiteral.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: A backslash-escaped character, e.g. \ (escaped space).

member.name: T:PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.WordPart.SingleQuoted
member.summary: A single-quoted string, e.g. 'hello world'.

member.name: M:PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.WordPart.SingleQuoted.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: A single-quoted string, e.g. 'hello world'.

member.name: T:PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.WordPart.DoubleQuoted
member.summary: A double-quoted string containing word parts, e.g. "hello $name".

member.name: M:PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.WordPart.DoubleQuoted.#ctor(System.Collections.Immutable.ImmutableArray{PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.WordPart})
member.summary: A double-quoted string containing word parts, e.g. "hello $name".

member.name: T:PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.WordPart.SimpleVarSub
member.summary: A simple variable substitution, e.g. $foo or $?.

member.name: M:PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.WordPart.SimpleVarSub.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: A simple variable substitution, e.g. $foo or $?.

member.name: T:PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.WordPart.BracedVarSub
member.summary: A braced variable substitution, e.g. ${foo:-default}.

member.name: M:PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.WordPart.BracedVarSub.#ctor(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: A braced variable substitution, e.g. ${foo:-default}.

member.name: T:PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.WordPart.CommandSub
member.summary: A command substitution, e.g. $(cmd) or `cmd`.

member.name: M:PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.WordPart.CommandSub.#ctor(PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.BashNode)
member.summary: A command substitution, e.g. $(cmd) or `cmd`.

member.name: T:PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.WordPart.ArithSub
member.summary: An arithmetic substitution, e.g. $(( x + 1 )).

member.name: M:PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.WordPart.ArithSub.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: An arithmetic substitution, e.g. $(( x + 1 )).

member.name: T:PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.WordPart.TildeSub
member.summary: A tilde substitution, e.g. ~ or ~user.

member.name: M:PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.WordPart.TildeSub.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: A tilde substitution, e.g. ~ or ~user.

member.name: T:PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.WordPart.GlobPart
member.summary: A glob pattern fragment: *, ?, [abc], or an extglob like +(*.py|*.js). The holds the raw text.

member.name: M:PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.WordPart.GlobPart.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: A glob pattern fragment: *, ?, [abc], or an extglob like +(*.py|*.js). The holds the raw text.

member.name: T:PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.WordPart.BracedTuple
member.summary: A brace expansion with comma-separated items, e.g. {a,b,c}. Each item is a string literal.

member.name: M:PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.WordPart.BracedTuple.#ctor(System.Collections.Immutable.ImmutableArray{System.String})
member.summary: A brace expansion with comma-separated items, e.g. {a,b,c}. Each item is a string literal.

member.name: T:PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.WordPart.BracedRange
member.summary: A brace expansion with a numeric range, e.g. {1..10}, {01..05}, or {1..10..2}. When is greater than zero, values are left-padded with zeros. When is non-zero, the range increments by that value.

member.name: M:PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.WordPart.BracedRange.#ctor(System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: A brace expansion with a numeric range, e.g. {1..10}, {01..05}, or {1..10..2}. When is greater than zero, values are left-padded with zeros. When is non-zero, the range increments by that value.

member.name: T:PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.WordPart.ProcessSub
member.summary: A process substitution, e.g. <(cmd) (input) or >(cmd) (output). The is the parsed command inside the substitution. is true for <(...), false for >(...).

member.name: M:PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.WordPart.ProcessSub.#ctor(PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.BashNode,System.Boolean)
member.summary: A process substitution, e.g. <(cmd) (input) or >(cmd) (output). The is the parsed command inside the substitution. is true for <(...), false for >(...).

member.name: T:PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.CompoundWord
member.summary: A compound word made up of one or more word parts. Modeled after oils syntax.asdl CompoundWord.

member.name: M:PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.CompoundWord.#ctor(System.Collections.Immutable.ImmutableArray{PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.WordPart})
member.summary: A compound word made up of one or more word parts. Modeled after oils syntax.asdl CompoundWord.

member.name: T:PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.ArrayWord
member.summary: An array literal value, e.g. (a b c) in arr=(a b c). Stored as a list of compound words representing each element.

member.name: M:PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.ArrayWord.#ctor(System.Collections.Immutable.ImmutableArray{PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.CompoundWord})
member.summary: An array literal value, e.g. (a b c) in arr=(a b c). Stored as a list of compound words representing each element.

member.name: T:PsBash.Core.Parser.BashLexer
member.summary: Hand-rolled lexer for bash input. Produces a flat token list from a string. Context-sensitive aspects (here-docs, alias expansion) are deferred to the parser.

member.name: M:PsBash.Core.Parser.BashLexer.Tokenize(System.String)
member.summary: Tokenize the given bash input into a list of tokens. The final token is always .

member.name: M:PsBash.Core.Parser.BashLexer.IsReservedWord(System.String)
member.summary: Returns true if the given word is a bash reserved word.

member.name: M:PsBash.Core.Parser.BashLexer.IsBraceExpansion(System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: Returns true if position starts a brace expansion pattern: { followed by content containing , or .., ending with }, with no unquoted whitespace inside.

member.name: M:PsBash.Core.Parser.BashLexer.TryReclassifyIoNumber(System.Collections.Generic.List{PsBash.Core.Parser.BashToken},System.Int32)
member.summary: If the last token is a Word consisting entirely of digits and we're about to emit a redirect operator, reclassify it as IoNumber.
Reclassify the last token as an IoNumber if it is a digit-only word immediately adjacent to the redirect operator (no whitespace between). is the position of the redirect operator.

member.name: T:PsBash.Core.Parser.ParseException
member.summary: Exception thrown when the bash parser encounters unsupported or invalid syntax. Includes source location (line/column) and the parser rule that failed.

member.name: M:PsBash.Core.Parser.ParseException.ComputeLineCol(System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: Compute 1-based line and column from a zero-based character offset in the input.

member.name: T:PsBash.Core.Parser.BashParser
member.summary: Hand-rolled recursive descent parser for bash input. Consumes the flat token list produced by and builds an AST.

member.name: M:PsBash.Core.Parser.BashParser.Parse(System.String)
member.summary: Parse the given bash input into an AST command node. Returns null if the input is empty or contains only whitespace/comments.

member.name: M:PsBash.Core.Parser.BashParser.ParseCompoundBody(System.String[])
member.summary: Parse a sequence of commands until one of the stop words is seen. Returns a single command or a CommandList.

member.name: M:PsBash.Core.Parser.BashParser.SkipTerminators
member.summary: Skip semicolons and newlines (used between compound command parts).

member.name: M:PsBash.Core.Parser.BashParser.CollectArithClause
member.summary: Collect tokens for one clause of a C-style for loop's arithmetic expression. Stops at Semi (consumes it) or RParen (consumed to close the (( ))).

member.name: M:PsBash.Core.Parser.BashParser.ConsumeCasePattern
member.summary: Consume a single case pattern. The pattern may contain glob chars like *. Stops at Pipe or RParen.

member.name: M:PsBash.Core.Parser.BashParser.ParseCaseBody
member.summary: Parse commands inside a case arm until ;; or esac is seen. Only skips newlines between commands, not semicolons (to detect ;; delimiter).

member.name: M:PsBash.Core.Parser.BashParser.IsParensFunctionDef
member.summary: Check whether the current position starts a name() { ... } function definition. Requires Word LParen RParen ahead without consuming tokens.

member.name: M:PsBash.Core.Parser.BashParser.ParseBraceGroup
member.summary: Parse a brace group: { commands }. Used for function bodies and standalone brace groups.

member.name: M:PsBash.Core.Parser.BashParser.CollectHereDocBody(System.String,System.Boolean,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Collect the body lines of a here-document from the token stream. Consumes tokens after the current line's newline until the delimiter is found on its own line.

member.name: M:PsBash.Core.Parser.BashParser.ParseAssignmentWordWithArray
member.summary: Parse an assignment word, consuming a trailing array literal (a b c) if present.

member.name: M:PsBash.Core.Parser.BashParser.DecomposeWord(System.String)
member.summary: Sub-parse a WORD token's raw text into typed WordPart children. Handles single quotes, double quotes, backslash escapes, $VAR references, and bare literal text.

member.name: M:PsBash.Core.Parser.BashParser.ContainsBracketGlob(System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: Returns true if position starts a bracket glob class (i.e. [ followed eventually by ]).

member.name: M:PsBash.Core.Parser.BashParser.IsBraceExpansionStart(System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: Returns true if position starts a brace expansion: { followed by content containing , or .., ending with }.

member.name: T:PsBash.Core.Parser.BashTokenKind
member.summary: The kind of a lexical token produced by .

member.name: T:PsBash.Core.Parser.BashToken
member.summary: A single lexical token from bash input.
member.param: The raw text of the token.
The zero-based character offset in the input.

member.name: M:PsBash.Core.Parser.BashToken.#ctor(PsBash.Core.Parser.BashTokenKind,System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: A single lexical token from bash input.
member.param: The raw text of the token.
The zero-based character offset in the input.

member.name: P:PsBash.Core.Parser.BashToken.Kind
member.summary: The token classification.

member.name: P:PsBash.Core.Parser.BashToken.Value
member.summary: The raw text of the token.

member.name: P:PsBash.Core.Parser.BashToken.Position
member.summary: The zero-based character offset in the input.

member.name: T:PsBash.Core.Parser.PsEmitter
member.summary: Walks parsed AST nodes and emits equivalent PowerShell strings.

member.name: F:PsBash.Core.Parser.PsEmitter._loopVars
member.summary: Emit PowerShell for the given command AST node.
Set of loop variable names currently in scope. Variables declared by for-in or for-arith loops emit as $var rather than $env:var.

member.name: F:PsBash.Core.Parser.PsEmitter._loopDepth
member.summary: Tracks nesting depth for generating unique loop iteration counter variable names.

member.name: F:PsBash.Core.Parser.PsEmitter.PsBuiltinAliases
member.summary: Commands that have PowerShell built-in aliases and should NOT be mapped to Invoke-Bash* when used as standalone (non-piped) commands. These work fine via PS aliases; only their piped forms need mapping.

member.name: M:PsBash.Core.Parser.PsEmitter.Transpile(System.String)
member.summary: Parse bash input and emit equivalent PowerShell. Returns null if the input is empty or whitespace-only.

member.name: M:PsBash.Core.Parser.PsEmitter.CollectLocalVars(PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.Command)
member.summary: Recursively collect variable names declared with 'local' in a command tree.

member.name: M:PsBash.Core.Parser.PsEmitter.ArithVarRef(System.String)
member.summary: Return the PowerShell variable reference for a bare name: loop vars and PS builtins get $name, others get $env:name.

member.name: M:PsBash.Core.Parser.PsEmitter.PrefixBareVar(System.String)
member.summary: Prefix bare variable names in arithmetic expressions with the correct PowerShell variable form. Loop variables (tracked in _loopVars) become $var; PS builtins keep $var; all other user variables become $env:var. Skips identifiers preceded by $ or - (PowerShell operators).

member.name: M:PsBash.Core.Parser.PsEmitter.EmitCondition(PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.Command)
member.summary: Emit a condition expression for use inside if/elif. BoolExpr nodes are unwrapped (no outer parens); plain commands are emitted as-is.

member.name: M:PsBash.Core.Parser.PsEmitter.EmitTestArg(PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.CompoundWord)
member.summary: Emit a word used as a test expression argument, unwrapping outer double quotes so that variable references appear bare (e.g. $HOME not "$HOME").

member.name: M:PsBash.Core.Parser.PsEmitter.TranslateHereDocVars(System.String)
member.summary: Translate bash variable references ($VAR, ${VAR}) in heredoc body text to PowerShell equivalents ($env:VAR).

member.name: M:PsBash.Core.Parser.PsEmitter.EmitAssignmentValue(PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.CompoundWord)
member.summary: Emit a value in assignment context: bare literals get wrapped in double quotes, already-quoted values are emitted as-is.

member.name: M:PsBash.Core.Parser.PsEmitter.TryEmitMappedCommand(PsBash.Core.Parser.Ast.Command.Simple,System.String@)
member.summary: Tries to emit a mapped PowerShell equivalent for known bash pipe-target commands. Returns false if the command is not a recognized pipe target.

member.name: M:PsBash.Core.Runtime.ModuleExtractor.ExtractEmbedded
member.summary: Extracts the embedded PsBash module to a temp directory and returns the path to PsBash.psd1. Uses a version-stamped directory so concurrent processes don't conflict. Thread-safe: uses a lock file to serialize extraction across processes.

member.name: M:PsBash.Core.Runtime.ModuleExtractor.ComputeEmbeddedHash(System.Reflection.Assembly)
member.summary: Computes a SHA256 hash over all embedded module resources.

member.name: M:PsBash.Core.Runtime.ModuleExtractor.WaitForMarker(System.String)
member.summary: Wait for another process to finish extraction (up to 10 seconds).

member.name: P:PsBash.Core.Runtime.PwshWorker.OutputCallback
member.summary: Optional callback that receives each output line. When set, output is routed to this callback instead of .

member.name: M:PsBash.Core.Runtime.PwshWorker.BuildInitScript
member.summary: Build the combined init script that loads the module in-memory and starts the command loop. Sent via stdin — no temp files required. NOTE: The worker loop below must stay in sync with scripts/ps-bash-worker.ps1 (used by the legacy -File path and e2e tests).

member.name: T:PsBash.Core.Transpiler.BashTranspiler
member.summary: Transpiles bash commands to equivalent PowerShell. This is the recommended entry point for library consumers.

member.name: M:PsBash.Core.Transpiler.BashTranspiler.Transpile(System.String)
member.summary: Transpile a bash command string to PowerShell.
member.exception: Thrown when the bash input cannot be parsed.
